name: GOLFTEC Ellicott City
address: 5725 Richards Valley Rd a5, Ellicott City, MD 21043
about: GOLFTEC Ellicott City is a golf training facility focused on data-driven instruction. It uses technology like OPTIMOTION swing tracking and high-speed video analysis to help golfers of all skill levels improve their swing mechanics. Lessons are personalized, beginning with a Swing Evaluation to create a custom game plan, and are led by PGA-certified coaches. The venue also offers club fitting services, using launch monitors to match players with equipment that optimizes distance and accuracy. While the specific Ellicott City page is unavailable, this description is based on the core GOLFTEC model of technology-enhanced golf improvement.
why_golfers_like_it: Personalized instruction using advanced motion measurement technology and data analysis
Comprehensive approach covering lessons, practice, and custom club fitting
Access to certified PGA coaches and a proven improvement system

food_and_drink.notes: No information on food service is available.
No information on alcohol service is available.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: While you can bring your own clubs, GOLFTEC also provides club rentals and specializes in custom club fitting to help you find the perfect equipment for your game.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: There is no information provided regarding outside food or drink policies. It is recommended to check with the center directly for specific details on bringing personal items.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes on a simulator?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes on a simulator can vary, but at GOLFTEC, the focus is on structured practice and lessons. The time would depend on the specific training plan or simulator package you book.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly? Are there junior programs?
faq.answer: Yes, GOLFTEC is suitable for kids and offers junior programs. They use technology and easy-to-follow lessons to help young golfers understand and improve their swing mechanics.
